text.title: AWS LLM Prompts Core Rules
text.summary: 5+ testable rules grounding the methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale. Includes the skip-this-methodology rule that the decision-tree maps to.

rule.statement: Prompts MUST start with a context-elicitation block listing: workload shape, latency target, request profile, data shape, region, compliance constraints, budget ceiling. Free-form `design me an AWS architecture` prompts are FORBIDDEN.

rationale.source: Prompt-engineering practice; Anthropic system prompt docs

rule.rationale: LLMs generate plausible-but-generic designs when context is absent. Structured context elicitation produces actionable designs the operator can evaluate.

rule.statement: Every prompt MUST declare a JSON output schema (components list, cost estimate, trade-offs list, risks list). LLM responses MUST be validated against the schema before downstream consumption.

rationale.source: Structured output patterns; Anthropic tool-use docs

rule.rationale: Free-form LLM responses break downstream automation. Schema-anchored output makes the response auditable and machine-consumable.

rule.statement: Constraints (compliance: SOC2/HIPAA/PCI; budget: $X/mo; region: ARN-prefix) MUST be pinned in the prompt before any design question. Designs that violate a pinned constraint are rejected on lint.
rule.rationale: LLMs over-fit on the most recent token; constraints buried mid-prompt are silently relaxed. Pinning constraints up-front + lint check enforces them.

rule.statement: Prompts and responses MUST NOT make compliance certification claims (HIPAA-certified, SOC2-certified, PCI-DSS-compliant). The LLM produces design proposals; compliance certification is an expert review process.
rule.rationale: LLM-generated compliance claims are wrong by default — the LLM cannot run the audit. Including such claims in output misleads downstream consumers.

rule.statement: Apply prompts to one architecture question per session. Bundled multi-question prompts MUST be split. Skip when the LLM is not configured with the project's own provider key (shared keys leak context across teams).
rule.rationale: Anti-conflation: bundled prompts get partial answers. Project-specific provider keys preserve context isolation and cost attribution.
